summary.intent: Add a generic webhooks system to the Moltis platform, supporting GitHub, GitLab, Stripe, and other provider triggers to run AI agents, with full settings UI, documentation, and proper navigation icons.
summary.outcome: Built a complete `moltis-webhooks` crate with 16 source files, 25 passing tests, gateway integration, HTTP ingress route, web UI settings page, and documentation. PR #575 created and pushed with follow-up commits for icon fix and agent/model select improvements.

repo: Webhooks crate follows the same pattern as `moltis-cron`: store trait + SQLite impl + service trait in service-traits + LiveXxxService in gateway + NoopXxxService for testing.

GatewayState fields for optional services (webhook_store, webhook_rate_limiter, webhook_worker_tx) are initialized to None/default in with_options; Arc::get_mut can be used right after creation (before any clones) to set them.
Public HTTP paths (no session auth required) are exempted in httpd/src/auth_middleware.rs — the pattern is to check the path prefix there.
RPC method registration happens in crates/gateway/src/methods/services.rs; read vs write auth split is in crates/gateway/src/methods/mod.rs.
The httpd crate has a pre-existing compile error under the vault feature (ssh_routes) that exists on main — not caused by this work.
Settings sidebar icons are defined as CSS classes (e.g., icon-globe, icon-activity) in a minified style.css; icon-link was already used by MCP causing the black square.
Agent and model selection in modals should use ComboSelect (from stores/model-store.js) and ModelSelect components with signals, not plain text inputs — established pattern in page-crons.js.
Gon data is populated in crates/web/src/templates.rs and serialized to the page; adding webhooks data required adding fields to the GonData struct and its construction site.

code.finding: Arc::get_mut used immediately after with_options() to set webhook_store, webhook_rate_limiter, and webhook_worker_tx before the Arc is shared anywhere.
Agent ID and Model Override fields converted from plain text inputs to ComboSelect/ModelSelect with signals to match cron page pattern.
Webhooks sidebar entry initially used icon-link (already used by MCP → black square); corrected to icon-globe.

workflow: Parallel agents were used extensively for large file creation tasks (core crate, store/auth, profiles) and exploration — effective for independent work.
Incremental compilation checks after each integration phase (crate alone → gateway → httpd) caught issues early.
Pre-existing errors on main should be verified with a git stash/checkout before assuming they are caused by current changes.
Plan files in plans/ directory are used as living design documents — improved before implementation begins.

friction: OAuth token expired at session start, requiring re-authentication before work could begin.
GatewayState is Arc-wrapped inside with_options(), making post-construction field mutation require Arc::get_mut workaround instead of a clean builder pattern.
Icon availability required grepping minified CSS to find usable class names; no icon reference/catalog exists.
The httpd vault feature pre-existing compile error caused confusion about whether our changes broke something — required verification against main.
open_items: GitLab, Linear, Jira, PagerDuty, and Sentry source profiles are planned in the design doc but only GitHub, GitLab, and Stripe profiles were created in the initial implementation — remaining providers need profiles.
Response action tools (post GitHub comments, merge PRs, issue Stripe refunds, etc.) are in the plan but not yet implemented — agents can receive events but cannot act back via typed tools.
Event filter UI (allow/deny checkboxes from provider event catalog) is described in the plan but the JS implementation uses free-form text; proper profile-aware checkbox UI deferred.
per_entity session mode (grouping events by entity key with serialized per-entity processing) is in the plan and types but worker implementation may not fully handle entity locking.
Webhook feature in CLI crate was explicitly skipped (deemed unnecessary since moltis-gateway pulls it in transitively) — should be verified this is actually correct.
Docs were written but not validated against the actual implementation (e.g., exact RPC method names, delivery status values) — may have minor discrepancies.
